content:
What is the Char Dham Yatra?

The Char Dham Yatra is the most sacred Hindu pilgrimage in northern India. The four dhams (sacred abodes) are Yamunotri (source of the Yamuna river), Gangotri (source of the Ganga river), Kedarnath (one of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva), and Badrinath (the abode of Lord Vishnu). All four temples are situated in the Garhwal region of Uttarakhand at altitudes between 3,000 and 3,600 metres in the Himalayas.

Hindus believe that completing the Char Dham Yatra at least once in a lifetime washes away sins and bestows moksha. The temples are open only during the summer and autumn months — typically from late April to early November — as they remain snowbound in winter.

Best Time for Char Dham Yatra

  • May to mid-June: Cool weather, full crowds, beautiful clear views. Hotels fill up fast — book 2-3 months ahead.
  • July-August: Heavy monsoon. Avoid due to landslide risks and trail closures.
  • September-October: Excellent weather, smaller crowds. Highly recommended for first-time yatris.

Char Dham Yatra Routes

The traditional Char Dham Yatra route is in clockwise order: Yamunotri → Gangotri → Kedarnath → Badrinath. Most pilgrims start from Haridwar (well-connected by train and air) and complete the circuit in 9-10 days. Total distance covered is approximately 1,750 km of mountain driving plus 50+ km of trekking.

Char Dham Yatra Registration 2026

The Uttarakhand government requires mandatory registration for all Char Dham pilgrims. Register at registrationandtouristcare.uk.gov.in. We help complete the registration as part of our service. Carry your registration certificate, government ID, and passport-size photographs.

Difficulty Level

The Char Dham Yatra includes three treks. Yamunotri is moderate (6 km one-way). Kedarnath is challenging (19-22 km one-way) but well-supported by ponies, palkis, and helicopters. Senior citizens are welcome — we routinely arrange helicopter ascents for elderly pilgrims.

What to Pack

Layered woollens, raincoat, sturdy trekking shoes, sunglasses, sunscreen, personal medicines, power bank, ID proof, and small daypack. Even in May, temperatures at Kedarnath can drop to 5°C. We share a detailed packing checklist after booking.

faq.q: Which Char Dham package is best for first-time pilgrims?
faq.a: We recommend the 9N/10D Char Dham Yatra. It includes all four dhams at a comfortable pace with proper rest days. The 8N/9D version is faster but more tiring. If you have less time, start with Do Dham (Kedarnath + Badrinath).

faq.q: Can I book Char Dham by helicopter?
faq.a: Yes. Full helicopter Char Dham Yatra (5-6 days, all four dhams by chopper) costs Rs 1,80,000-2,10,000 per person. We can arrange this. For a hybrid approach, do Kedarnath by helicopter and other dhams by road — saves time and is more affordable.

faq.q: How early should I book Char Dham Yatra 2026?
faq.a: Book at least 2-3 months in advance for May/June dates. Hotels in Kedarnath, Badrinath, Yamunotri area are limited and fill up fast. Helicopter bookings open in February-March. Last-minute bookings are possible but with limited hotel options.

faq.q: What is the difference between Char Dham and Chota Char Dham?
faq.a: Char Dham (or Chota Char Dham) refers to the four dhams in Uttarakhand — Yamunotri, Gangotri, Kedarnath, Badrinath. The original Char Dham (as established by Adi Shankaracharya) refers to four shrines across India: Badrinath (north), Dwarka (west), Puri (east), Rameshwaram (south). Most pilgrims today refer to the Uttarakhand Char Dham.

content:
What is the Golden Triangle?

The Golden Triangle refers to India's most popular tourist circuit connecting three cities — Delhi, Agra, and Jaipur. The three cities form a roughly equilateral triangle on the map, each about 200-250 km apart. Together they showcase the best of North Indian heritage, Mughal architecture, royal Rajput culture, and ancient history.

Why Visit the Golden Triangle?

  • Delhi: India's capital. Mughal monuments (Red Fort, Humayun's Tomb), British colonial architecture (India Gate, Rashtrapati Bhavan), and chaotic Old Delhi bazaars (Chandni Chowk).
  • Agra: Home of the Taj Mahal — one of the New Seven Wonders of the World. Also Agra Fort, Fatehpur Sikri, and beautiful Mehtab Bagh sunset view.
  • Jaipur: Capital of Rajasthan, the Pink City. Amber Fort, City Palace, Hawa Mahal, Jantar Mantar (UNESCO observatory), and vibrant bazaars selling gemstones, textiles, and blue pottery.

Best Time for Golden Triangle

October to March is ideal — pleasant weather (15-28°C), clear skies, and dry conditions. December and January can have morning fog in Delhi but temperatures are still comfortable. April-June is very hot (40-45°C) — sightseeing becomes difficult. July-September is monsoon season — humid but green; some travellers prefer this for fewer crowds.

Recommended Duration

The minimum recommended duration is 5 days / 4 nights. This allows 1.5 days in Delhi, 1 day in Agra, and 1.5 days in Jaipur with comfortable travel time. Adventurous travellers can do it in 4 days, but it will feel rushed. 7-8 days lets you explore at leisure and add side trips like Mathura/Vrindavan, Pushkar, or Ranthambore wildlife safari.

How Much Does It Cost?

Budget Golden Triangle tours start at Rs 14,500 per person. Mid-range comfort packages with 4-star hotels and AC vehicle are Rs 18,500-25,000. Luxury palace hotel tours range Rs 38,000-65,000. Per-person pricing depends on group size — solo travellers pay more, larger groups pay less per person.

faq.q: What's the best length for a Golden Triangle tour?
faq.a: 5 nights / 6 days is the sweet spot. You get enough time in each city without feeling rushed. 4 days is too tight. 7-8 days lets you add experiences like Mathura, Pushkar, or Ranthambore.

faq.q: Is the Taj Mahal closed any day?
faq.a: Yes — closed every Friday. Plan your Agra visit on any other day. Open from sunrise to sunset.

faq.q: Can foreigners book Golden Triangle tours easily?
faq.a: Absolutely. We specialise in handling foreign travellers — English-speaking guides, vegetarian and special diet meal options, airport pickups, visa documentation help. WhatsApp us with your dates.

faq.q: Can I add Varanasi or Rajasthan to the Golden Triangle?
faq.a: Yes — common extensions: +Varanasi (3 days, by overnight train or flight), +Pushkar+Udaipur (5 days), +Ranthambore tiger safari (2 days), +Khajuraho temples (2 days). We design custom itineraries.

content:
About the Taj Mahal

The Taj Mahal is a white marble mausoleum built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan between 1632 and 1648 in memory of his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal, who died in childbirth. It is widely considered the finest example of Mughal architecture and one of the most beautiful buildings ever constructed. The Taj is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.

Best Time to Visit the Taj Mahal

  • Sunrise (most magical): 6:00-9:00 AM. Soft golden light, fewer crowds, white marble glows pink-orange.
  • Late afternoon: 4:00-6:30 PM. Warm light, lengthening shadows.
  • Full moon nights: Special night viewing on full moon and 2 days before/after (limited tickets, advance booking required).

Taj Mahal Entry Tickets 2026

  • Indian citizens: Rs 50
  • Foreign tourists: Rs 1,100 (~$13 USD)
  • Mausoleum entry (additional): Rs 200
  • Children below 15: Free

Tickets can be bought online at the official ASI website to skip queues. The Taj Mahal is closed every Friday.

Distance from Major Cities

  • From Delhi: 230 km (3.5-4 hours by car via Yamuna Expressway, 1.5 hours by Gatimaan Express train)
  • From Jaipur: 240 km (4 hours by car)
  • From Mumbai: ~1,400 km (2 hour flight to Delhi/Agra)
  • From Varanasi: 600 km (10-11 hours by train, 1 hour flight via Delhi)

faq.q: How long should I spend at the Taj Mahal?
faq.a: 2-3 hours minimum to fully appreciate the Taj. This includes the entrance, the gardens, the mausoleum, and time for photographs. Longer (3-4 hours) if you want to read the Quranic inscriptions, examine the inlay work, or visit the Taj Mahal museum on the grounds.

faq.q: Is the Taj Mahal worth visiting?
faq.a: Absolutely. The Taj Mahal is consistently ranked among the most beautiful buildings in the world. The white marble inlay craftsmanship, the perfect symmetry, and the romantic backstory create an unforgettable experience. First-time visitors to India almost always rank it as the highlight of their trip.

faq.q: Can I take photos at the Taj Mahal?
faq.a: Yes — outside in the gardens. Inside the mausoleum, photography is prohibited. Tripods, professional video cameras, and food are not allowed. Standard cameras and phones are fine. The classic photo spot is the central pool reflection — go early morning to avoid crowds.

faq.q: Is there parking at the Taj Mahal?
faq.a: There's no parking near the monument due to pollution restrictions. Visitors are dropped at a parking area 1 km away and use battery-operated buses or walk to the entrance. Our tour includes this transfer.
